books.title: The Secret Garden
books.author: Frances Hodgson Burnett
books.shortIntro: A gentle first-night classic with soft movement, familiar language, and a warm sense of discovery.
books.detailBody: For bedtime listening, this title works best when it feels approachable instead of endless. The language is familiar, the emotional motion stays soft, and the opening chapters are forgiving if a listener drifts off before the end.

bedtime.supportLine: A calming first-night classic when you want something familiar and low pressure.

parts.title: Night 1: Arrival at Misselthwaite
parts.synopsis: Mary Lennox arrives at Misselthwaite Manor, where the first hints of quiet change begin to stir around her.

parts.title: Night 2: The Locked Door
parts.synopsis: Mary grows more curious about the house and the rumors of a hidden place sealed away from sight.

parts.title: Night 3: The Robin Leads the Way
parts.synopsis: A small robin begins to pull Mary out of herself, nudging her closer to the garden's mystery.

books.title: The Wind in the Willows
books.author: Kenneth Grahame
books.shortIntro: Soft river scenes and easy companionship keep the mood settled from beginning to end.
books.detailBody: This is a strong bedtime listen when you want atmosphere more than momentum. The pleasure comes from returning to familiar company and letting the scenes move at a patient pace.

bedtime.supportLine: A low-pressure listen for nights when you want texture, companionship, and almost no urgency.

parts.title: Night 1: The River Bank
parts.synopsis: Mole leaves his spring cleaning behind and discovers the softer rhythms of the river with Rat.

parts.title: Night 2: The Open Road
parts.synopsis: The world grows slightly wider, but the voice stays unhurried and companionable.

parts.title: Night 3: The Piper at the Gates
parts.synopsis: The most dreamlike section of the book, shaped for a quieter descent into sleep.

books.title: Heidi
books.author: Johanna Spyri
books.accentLabel: A story for a few nights
books.shortIntro: A warm mountain story with clear nightly stopping points and a gentle return to familiar scenes.
books.detailBody: Heidi fits the multi-night promise well because the world is easy to re-enter and each section can stop on a calm breath. It gives continuity without turning bedtime into homework.

bedtime.supportLine: A reassuring multi-night listen with enough continuity to feel rewarding and enough repetition to feel safe.

parts.title: Night 1: Up the Mountain
parts.synopsis: Heidi arrives in the mountains, where the first emotional notes are simple, open-air, and calm.

parts.title: Night 2: Grandfather's Hut
parts.synopsis: The rhythms of mountain life settle in, building familiarity rather than tension.

parts.title: Night 3: Pastures and Goat Bells
parts.synopsis: The story deepens through repetition and place, which makes it easy to return to on the next night.

parts.title: Night 4: A New City
parts.synopsis: The setting changes, but the adaptation keeps the transitions gentle and easy to follow.

books.title: Jane Eyre
books.author: Charlotte Bronte
books.accentLabel: A longer book to settle into
books.shortIntro: A fuller classic experience broken into bedtime-sized portions so it feels steady rather than endless.
books.detailBody: The long-form mode matters here: the adaptation gives Jane Eyre enough room to breathe while still shaping each night around a clean stopping point and a low-friction return path.

bedtime.supportLine: A slow-burn classic for listeners who want continuity, atmosphere, and a dependable way back in each night.

parts.title: Night 1: Gateshead
parts.synopsis: Jane's early world is established with enough clarity to support a longer reading relationship.

parts.title: Night 2: Lowood
parts.synopsis: The adaptation softens the harsher edges while preserving the emotional shape of Jane's growth.

parts.title: Night 3: Thornfield Hall
parts.synopsis: The story widens into a house and atmosphere you can return to over many nights.

parts.title: Night 4: Questions in the Hall
parts.synopsis: Curiosity is present, but the pacing remains measured so it still works close to sleep.

parts.title: Night 5: What Jane Keeps
parts.synopsis: The arc resolves with enough emotional closure to make the book feel completed rather than abandoned.
